title: Security Scenarios
drDigitalIntro:
Let's see if you can handle real security situations!
• Create a strong password, handle two-factor authentication, and spot dangerous messages.
• Take your time and think before you click.

playgroundTask.goal: You handled passwords, 2FA, and identified scams

steps.say: Create a strong password. Type one that's at least 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols.
Now sign in to a website. Type your username: digitallearner
Type the password you just created to log in.
A 2FA code was sent to your phone. Enter the code shown.
Click Verify to confirm your code.
Look at this message — is it safe or dangerous?
steps.target: Your account has been compromised! Click here NOW: http://amaz0n-security.tk/verify

steps.say: That link looks suspicious. Mark it as dangerous.

steps.target: Hi! Here's the shared Google Doc you asked for: https://docs.google.com/d/abc123

steps.say: This one's from someone you know, with a real Google link. Mark it as safe.
Try the passkey option for a faster, safer login.

drDigitalSuccess: You passed every security test! Strong passwords, 2FA, spotting scams, and using passkeys — you're ready to stay safe online.
drDigitalHint: Make passwords long and complex, always verify 2FA codes, and check URLs carefully before clicking.
